master.heading: MASTER
master.title: Master training
master.mainText: The activities of MNUMS graduate education are regulated by the PROCEDURES REGULATING THE GRADUATE COURSE ACTIVITIES OF THE MONGOLINA NATIONAL UNIVERSITY OF MEDICAL SCIENCES. Admission to master's and doctoral courses is held twice a year. /Spring between February 1-15, autumn between August 25 and September 10/ The student will write and defend a master's thesis according to the sequence below.

list.item1: Must have studied 10 credits of core courses
list.item2: Department meeting: (The department will announce the meeting)
list.item2-3: Assigning a research supervisor and advisor
list.item3: Attend a meeting of the Research Ethics Committee and obtain ethics approval
list.item4: Must have studied 12 credits of major courses
list.item6: Progress discussion will be held once at the department meeting
list.item7: Attend Research Ethics Committee meetings and make ethical decisions
list.item8: Entry into preliminary defense: (specified in section 4.2 of the PROCEDURE FOR REGULATING GRADUATE TRAINING ACTIVITIES)
list.item9: Entry into preliminary protection: (as specified in section 4.2 of the PROCEDURE FOR REGULATING GRADUATE EDUCATIONAL ACTIVITIES
list.item9-3: for a list of materials to be submitted by a preliminary defense, who successfully pass.
list.item9-6: to register after the defense Students who have successfully defended their Master's degree.

subjectList.subtitle: Master training
subjectList.item1: International Cyber Education Center at Graduate School, Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences (MNUMS) has been established on October 15, 2015 as a pioneering entirely online distance learning graduate education provider for master’s degree programme’s in the Mongolian higher education system. We operated as International Cyber University of Medical Sciences during our inaugural years.
subjectList.item2: Professor Young Moon Chae, honorary professor of MNUMS, visiting professor of Yonsei University in South Korea played enormous role in establishing distance learning platform that is fully operating for a graduate curriculum in Mongolia, with his expertise in developing digital governance and health information system in Asia and Western Pacific region.
subjectList.item3: We work to promote health professionals’ education via learning management system. E-learning has been rapidly evolving at the global stage along with technological advances in recent years, coupled with busy healthcare professionals’ endless needs for continuous medical education and professional growth. Currently we are offering 8 distinct graduate programme’s entirely online for master’s degree, these include as follows.

subjectList1.item8: Methods of creating and presenting scientific works

subjectList2.item4: Methods of Clinical Research in Traditional Medicine

subjectList3.item8: Evaluation of ED projects and bills

subjectList3.item3: The nature, role and development of the nurse specialist

subjectList2.item1: Medicinal materials used in oral treatment-II
subjectList2.item3: Marketing and Management of Oral Aids

subjectList3.item3: Diseases of supporting tissues of teeth, inflammation of soft and root apex

doctor.heading: Doctor
doctor.title: Doctoral training
doctor.mainText: The activities of MNUMS graduate education are regulated by the PROCEDURES REGULATING THE GRADUATE COURSE ACTIVITIES OF THE MONGOLIAN NATIONAL UNIVERSITY OF MEDICAL SCIENCES. Admission to master's and Ph.D courses has 2 stages.
doctor.subText: The research work shall be carried out in accordance with clause 7.12 of the regulations regulating the activities of doctoral graduate studies. It includes:

list.item1: Meeting of department that belongs professional fields:
list.item1-1: To assign Research topics, Research methods, Research supervisors and advisors.
list.item2-1: To approve research topics, research methods, research supervisors and advisors.
list.item3: Obtain permission to start research at the meeting of the Research Ethics Committee:
list.item3-1: Requires the publication of two or more articles where you are the first author, under the affiliation or name of MNUMS, in peer-reviewed professional international journals. One of these articles must be published in a journal indexed in recognized databases such as Web of Science, Scopus, PubMed, or Central Asian Journal of Medical Sciences.
list.item3-2: Requires the publishing two or more articles based on research results in domestic journals. One of these articles, as specified, should be published in the Journal of Health Sciences.
list.item5: You need to have participated in the meeting or journal club that related department.
list.item7: To confirm the results of the research process.
list.item8: At the meeting of the research ethics committee, the conclusion that the research ethics norms were not violated:
list.item10: Evaluation of the Probability Expert Commission Team:

list.item13: Defense of the degree of Ph.D in Medicine.

list.item15-2: to download the revision template file.

subjectList.subtitle: MNUMS Post-doctoral Training
subjectList.item1: The number of post-doctoral researchers will be determined and selected at the meeting of the leaders of the Academic Council in accordance with the research priorities and implementation projects of MNUMS.
subjectList.item2: Post-doctoral studies are 1-2 years. Based on the results of the study, it can be extended for up to 1 year.
subjectList.item3: Scholars from foreign and domestic universities, academic and research institutions in cooperation with MNUMS can participate.
subjectList.item4: It will be based on departments, laboratories, institutes and university hospitals of MNUMS.
subjectList.item5: The Research Center will sign a tri-party agreement with the post-doctoral researcher and the supervisor and monitor the implementation of the agreement.
subjectList.item6: Post-doctoral researchers and supervisors will report the results of the research progress to the Research Center every six months. The report of the research project will be discussed and handed over to the Academic Council of MNUMS. The post-doctoral researcher must publish 1 article as the first or responsible author in a journal registered in the Web of Science, Scopus, and Pubmed databases.
subjectList.item7: Under the supervision of the relevant department and supervisor, will guide the teaching and research work of master's and doctoral students.
subjectList.item8: The post-doctoral researcher will be paid at the faculty level.
subjectList.item9: In the event of breach of contract by the researcher, all costs related to post-doctoral research funding will be reimbursed.
subjectList.item10: A post-doctoral researcher is not allowed to engage in dual employment outside the university.

library.title: LIBRARY SERVICES
library.body: The Central Library of Health Sciences serves as a comprehensive resource centre for students, researchers, and medical professionals. It provides access to a wide range of books, research materials, and electronic resources to support academic and professional development. In the last academic year, the library expanded its collection with over 6,700 new books and 1,400 e-books.
library.room4: Methods of creating and presenting scientific works

institutes.title: INSTITUTE
institutes.body1: On March 27, 2023, the Graduate School of ASHIUS, Division of Graduate Policy  and Educational Management, and the Central Library of Health Sciences jointly organized the 'DAY FOR STUDENTS IN GRADUATE COURSES OF ASHUIS' on March 27, 2023 for master's and doctoral students. The day was planned to assist in the identification of research subjects and research expenses, as well as to provide prospects for collaborative research effort. It was arranged with the assistance of ASHUIS institutes, the medical molecular diagnostics center, and some companies. 128 master's and doctoral students of ASHIUS participated in the day. 17 master's students and 5 doctoral students from ASHIUS  institutes, medical molecular diagnostic centers and companies were openly announced and presented as researchers for the research project. In addition, two researcher positions have been announced. Graduate and doctoral students who are available for the project may be employed.
institutes.body2: B. Tsogtbaatar, M.D  Ph.D, senior expert of the UN Children's Fund, attended the event, and a guest talk on 'Informed decision-making' was presented. The Graduate School's Department of Medical Research, the CAJMS journal, and research for 82 students gave assistance and information on work methodology and biostatistics. In addition, approximately 90 students obtained a library card, downloaded the no-cost Endnote application, and visited the dissertation library.
institutes.body3: The organizers would like to convey our heartfelt gratitude to the Institute of Medicine, Institute of Biomedicine, Institute of Public Health in ASHIUS, Center for Medical Molecular Diagnostics, Altanburd Ltd. for providing numerous chances to graduate students.

researchDay.title: ACADEMIC CONFERENCE
researchDay.body1: MNUMS hosts an annual Academic Conference every April, providing a platform for graduate students, faculty, and researchers to present and discuss their research results. The 66th conference is scheduled for 2024. The event showcases a wide range of research projects, with the highest-rated projects selected for oral presentations. The conference not only encourages academic exchange but also recognizes excellence through the Master's Award, which is presented to the best research project. This conference offers valuable opportunities for networking, feedback, and collaboration within the academic community.
researchDay.body2: In 2024, the 66th conference was held, and in the graduate studies session, 15 online master's students submitted research projects and abstracts. The top 3 projects were selected for oral presentation, and ICEC master's student Sossorbarm was awarded the BEST ONLINE MASTER'S STUDENT award for the project "Analysis of Innovation Policy in the Pharmaceutical Sector of Mongolia."
researchDay.body3: Examining the Results of Mesenchymal Stem Cell Therapy for Shoulder Ligament Tears by Ganbolor, a Master's Student at the Online Master, Presents the Research Project and answering Researchers' Questions.
researchDay.body4: Presenting the Research Project of E. Enkhjargal, a Master's Student at the Online Master, Investigating the Validation of Multidisciplinary Tests in the Diagnosis of Chronic Anemic Heart Disease.

sportDay.title: STUDENT DAY & OTHER EVENTS
sportDay.subtitle: Sports Competition
sportDay.body1: Student Day is an annual event organised to bridge the gap between students and potential employers. It offers a platform for students to network with industry professionals, learn about career opportunities, and explore internships or job placements. In addition, the event features employer-driven projects, allowing students to participate in real-world initiatives and gain hands-on experience. Student Day also fosters collaboration between the university, businesses, and other organizations, creating valuable connections that enhance students' career prospects.
sportDay.body2: The MNUMS sports team actively participated in the Sports Competition, showcasing their skills and sportsmanship. The Graduate School, in collaboration with the International Students Office, successfully organised a joint sports competition, bringing together students from both local and international backgrounds to compete in a variety of events.

journal.title: JOURNAL CLUB
journal.subtitle: The regular Journal club of the Graduate school was organized.
journal.subtitleBody: A journal club that offers chances developed is organized by providing fresh material in the field of recent academic and research, medical, and discussing evidence-based findings.
journal.body1: Journal club is organized on the first week of every month by the Health Research Department of the Graduate School.
journal.body3: The study was published in a peer-reviewed journal with IF-2,3 in 2022, but it is also unique in that it was jointly performed by researchers from Mongolia, Japan, and Indonesia.
journal.body2: The Graduate School organizes Journal Club to train Master’s and Doctoral students in the methodology of academic research within the health sciences. This initiative aims to empower students, enhance the quality of their research, and create opportunities for collaborative discussions between students and faculty. By participating in the Journal Club, students will have the opportunity to:
journal.item1: Evaluate and refine their research projects and thesis, gaining valuable feedback from peers and faculty to improve their scientific work.
journal.item2: Engage in discussions on international research, broadening their understanding of global trends, innovations, and challenges in the health sciences.
journal.item3: Explore various research methods and learn how to apply these techniques to enhance the rigor and impact of their own research.
journal.item4: Through these activities, the Journal Club serves as both a learning platform and a forum for intellectual exchange, fostering the development of critical thinking and research skills.

dormitory.title: DORMITORY
dormitory.subtitle: MNUMS offers student housing for both domestic and international students. The accommodation includes furnished rooms, shared bathrooms, and common areas such as a kitchen, laundry room, and study spaces.
dormitory.body1: There are five student dormitories, one for international students and scholars with capacity of 600 persons and one others for local students with capacity of 600 persons. It is a safe place to live and rooms are furnished. Student affairs and complaints are resolved by a dormitory manager.
dormitory.body2: International students contact the Student and Alumni Affairs office and the office of university registrar in advance to arrange a room at the dormitory. As the dormitory for international students has a limited number of rooms. Earlier booking is advised. The dormitory is guaranteed for government funded students and incoming exchange students. The self-funded students may apply for the dormitory.

The duration of registration is not less than 5 working days.

subjectList.subtitle: Requirements for Entrants: (Regulation 2.3)
subjectList.item1: 2.3.1 Applicants for master's and doctoral programme’s must meet the standards of the Academic School's admission examination developed within the context of the detailed investigation.
subjectList.item2: 2.3.2 Master's program applicants must have a bachelor's degree or an equivalent degree, as well as a bachelor's grade point average of at least 2.5 (75%).
subjectList.item3: 2.3.3 Doctoral program applicants must have a master's degree in science or an equivalent degree with a master's grade point average of at least 3.0 (80%).
subjectList.item4: 2.3.4 If the applicant enrolls in a master's or doctoral program in a different field (in-depth study) than the one he or she has previously mastered, he or she must study the gap to ensure course content consistency. Remedial courses do not count toward the program's core hours.
subjectList.item5: 2.3.5 A international student who chooses to study Mongolian has fulfilled the Mongolian language requirements. The students will abide by the terms and circumstances of the intergovernmental agreement, according to the agreement.
subjectList.item6: 2.3.6 An international student who chooses an English-language study program must complete the admission exam criteria using content created collaboratively by the foreign language center and the Academic School. Special requirements for applicants may be imposed based on the suggestions of the component schools, professional departments, and units, depending on the content and characteristics of the particular research program.

greeting.title: Dean's Greeting
greeting.greeting1: In this era of rapid advancement in science and technology, the vision of the Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences (MNUMS) is to become one of the world’s leading universities. Guided by our mission to be a premier institution in academic research, higher education, and healthcare services, the university is committed to graduate training, research, innovation, and professional activities.
greeting.greeting2: The Graduate School, in collaboration with other constituent schools, implements 11 graduate programme’s and offers online master's program in 6 fields, providing advanced-level education to domestic and international physicians and healthcare professionals. One unique feature of the online master's program is its ability to allow professionals to continue their studies while working and living in their respective locations. Notably, the online master's program in medicine has achieved international accreditation for the first time through ACQUIN, a recognized accreditation body.
greeting.greeting3: The Graduate School's faculty and staff are dedicated to supporting the academic and research activities of students, researchers, and faculty members. Our goal is to enrich medical science with new knowledge, transform these innovations into practical applications, and contribute to the development of Mongolia's knowledge-based economy.
greeting.greeting4: In terms of professional activities and social responsibility, the school conducts a wide range of initiatives. Special attention is given to international students participating in graduate programme’s by providing guidance on research methodology, development, and processing, as well as offering support in learning the Mongolian language. Additionally, we are expanding collaborations with healthcare and higher education institutions in the fields of medical law and medical education.
greeting.greeting5: The doors of the Graduate School are always open to anyone eager to delve into academia and enhance their education. Our skilled and experienced faculty members are ready to welcome you with respect and enthusiasm, collaborating with you in learning, developing, and creating together.
greeting.greeting6: May your academic endeavors be fruitful and abundant!
greeting.name: Shiirevnyamba Avirmed

aboutUs.title: About us
aboutUs.text1: The Graduate school was established under order A/135 of the President of the Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences (MNUMS) in 2018. It comprises the Department of Graduate Education and the Department of International Cyber Education and Academic council of Graduate school, Subcommittee of Programme.
aboutUs.text3: The Graduate School offers advanced professional training through 11 doctoral and on-campus master’s programmes, and 6 online master’s programmes.
aboutUs.text4: The Department of International Cyber Education, originally established on October 15, 2015, as the International Cyber University, was the first institution in Mongolia to offer fully online master’s programmes in graduate education in the field of medical sciences.
aboutUs.text5: The Graduate School operates with the following departments and unit:

departments.medical_law_education: Department of Medical Legislation and Education
departments.central_asian_journal: Central Asian Journal of Medical Sciences

aboutUs.text10: We organize a variety of events for graduate students outside the classroom, such as day events for students, international online learning day events, scientific conferences, and journal clubs. In March of every year, we held an event titled "GRADUATE STUDENT'S DAY" for master's and doctoral students. This event aimed to help students choose research topics, master research methodologies, and explore opportunities for collaborative research. It was jointly organized by the faculty of the School of Graduate Studies, four institutes of MNUMS, the Clinical Molecular Diagnostics Centre, and the Health Sciences Library. Faculties of Graduate school, and the editorial team of the official CAJMS journal of MNUMS provided guidance and information on research methodology and biostatistics to students (including online master’s students). During the event, about students registered for library cards, had the Endnote program installed for free, and explored the dissertation repository.

gepm.header: Division of Graduate Education Policy and Management
gepm.body: The Division of Graduate Education Policy and Management was established in 1992 under the name of the Doctoral and Master’s Office of the Health Science University of Mongolia (as it was then called) and began its operations under the direct supervision of the Deputy Director for Research and External Relations, consisting of a director, a scientific secretary of the Academic Council, and a methodologist. In 2020, by Resolution No. 002 of the Board of Directors of the MNUMS dated September 23, 2020, in connection with the new approval of the University of Health Sciences structure, the Graduate Education Policy and Management was established by the Director’s Order No. A/228 dated October 16, 2020, with the responsibility of organizing and supervising the implementation of master’s, doctoral training, and post-doctoral research, and is implementing the following areas of activity.
gepm.body2: To construct a research institution that combines teaching and academic research, and produces scientists and researchers who contribute to scientific information, knowledge, and innovation.

partnershipKyushu.title: Kyushu University
partnershipKyushu.body: Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences was signed with National University Corporation Kyushu University, Japan on student exchange and academic cooperations for exchange of academic staff, administrative staff and students; plan for joint research; exchange of academic information and publications; and other academic exchanges to which both Universities agree in August 1, 2024.

partnershipOita.title: Oita University
partnershipOita.body: Starting in the 2024-2025 academic year, the collaboration between the Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences (MNUMS) and Oita University, Japan, has expanded with the launch of a joint doctoral program. Graduates of this double degree program will receive doctorates from both institutions.
partnershipOita.body2: Professor Seigo Kitano, Director of Oita University, Japan, and Professor Shomei Ryozawa from the Department of Gastroenterology at Saitama University, met with MNUMS Deputy Director for Education Policy and Regulation at MNUMS, Ts. Bilegtsaikhan. The meeting included Director of the Department of Foreign Affairs, D. Ulziimaa; the Director of Graduate School, A. Shiirevnyamba; faculty from the Department of Gastroenterology; Oita University alumni; and representatives from the Department of Health Research. Discussions focused on the doctoral Double Degree Program (DDP), with the School of Medicine at Oita University and the Graduate School of MNUMS, agreeing to implement the program.
partnershipOita.body3: A MOU and agreement was signed to initiate the Double Degree Program (DDP) as part of the expanded partnership between MNUMS and Oita University. The signing ceremony was attended by key officials, including Rector of MNUMS, Professor  Damdindorj B.; the Deputy Director of Education Policy and Regulation at MNUMS, Ts. Bilegtsaikhan; the Director of the Department of Foreign Affairs, D. Ulziimaa; and the Director of Graduate School, Dr. A. Shiyrevnyamba. Representatives from Oita University, including President Dr. Seigo Kitano, Vice Director Dr. Yoshiyo Yamaoka, and Dr. Naohisa Yahagi from Keio University, also participated. Prior to the signing, discussions explored further collaboration between MNUMS and Oita University.
partnershipOita.body4: The Double Degree Program will admit up to five doctoral students per year, offering them the unique opportunity to graduate with diplomas from both universities. This program is the first of its kind in the field of medicine in Mongolia, providing a distinctive double-degree pathway for advanced medical studies.

partnershipLumiere.title: Lumiere, Lyon University
partnershipLumiere.body: Clauses 30.1.2 and 31.1.3 of the Law on Higher Education, Objective 2.4 of Mongolia's long-term development policy "Vision-2050", and Objective 3 of "Development Policy-2030" of the Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences, respectively based on this, a joint project between the Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences and the "Lumiere Lyon 2" University of France aims to increase international cooperation and improve information technology in the field of medical science education in Mongolia. The working group for the introduction of the master's (1+1) international curriculum has been established. The collaboration is a continuation of the European Union's Erasmus+ DiGiHealth-Asia project, "Strengthening the capacity of digital health monitoring and care systems in Asia". According to the working group, the Head of the Department of Health Research, Academic School, J. Jambaldorj, MD, Associate Professor, MD, Associate Professor Sh. Dr. E. Sarantuya and others are working.
partnershipLumiere.body2: As part of the introduction of the joint curriculum, professor Aicha Sekhari, professor Yacine Ouzrout, and associate professor Nathalie Auvergnon from "Lumiere Lyon 2" school came to MNUMS from October 12 to 15, 2024, and a meeting was held successfully and further work is proceeding according to plan.

healthResearch.title: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH RESEARCH
healthResearch.body1: To be a leading center of excellence in health research education, training, and innovation in Mongolia, fostering advanced methodologies and ethical standards to address national and global health challenges.
healthResearch.text1: Equip graduate students and researchers with comprehensive skills in research methodology, epidemiology, biostatistics, and research ethics to produce high-quality, evidence-based research.
healthResearch.text2: Advance scientific knowledge and innovation through rigorous training and research that address pressing health issues, improving public health outcomes locally and globally.
healthResearch.text3: Uphold ethical principles in research, fostering a responsible, accountable, and transparent research culture that respects the rights and dignity of all communities.
healthResearch.objective1_detail1: Offer a curriculum in research methodology, epidemiology, biostatistics, and ethics to prepare students for health research.
healthResearch.objective1_detail2: Develop programme’s that meet evolving public health, medical, and policy needs in Mongolia and beyond.
healthResearch.objective2_detail1: Foster innovative research that addresses Mongolia-specific health issues and contributes to global health knowledge.
healthResearch.objective2_detail2: Support students and researchers through all stages of the research process.
healthResearch.objective3_detail1: Uphold high ethical standards, ensuring integrity and transparency in all research.
healthResearch.objective3_detail2: Promote ethical practices to protect human rights, data confidentiality, and cultural respect.
healthResearch.objective4_detail1: Collaborate with public health entities to ensure research findings lead to actionable health solutions.
healthResearch.objective4_detail2: Support community health initiatives to improve population health and reduce disparities.
healthResearch.objective5_detail1: Partner with international institutions to improve research training quality.
healthResearch.objective5_detail2: Facilitate exchange programme’s for knowledge-sharing and global perspectives in health research.
healthResearch.faculty_head_specialization: Specialization: Molecular Biology, Genomics, Neurodegenerative Diseases
healthResearch.faculty_1_specialization: Epidemiology, Research Methodology, Editor in Diagnosis journal
healthResearch.faculty_3_specialization: Traditional Medical Doctor, Quality assurance management
healthResearch.course1_description: This subject introduces students to designing and conducting high-quality research, covering qualitative and quantitative methods, hypothesis formulation, study design, and data analysis. Students gain critical skills for evaluating research literature and creating robust studies that address health challenges.
healthResearch.course2_description: The Epidemiology course examines disease patterns and causes within populations. Students learn key concepts such as incidence, prevalence, and risk factors, equipping them to analyze health trends and develop preventive strategies. Practical applications include outbreak investigation and chronic disease epidemiology, essential for impactful public health research.
healthResearch.course3_description: Biostatistics provides students with the statistical tools necessary to analyze health data. Topics include probability, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis, with a focus on hands-on experience using statistical software. This course builds proficiency in data interpretation, crucial for evidence-based health research.
healthResearch.course4_description: Research Ethics focuses on ethical principles in health research, such as informed consent, confidentiality, and data privacy. Students explore case studies to understand ethical dilemmas and apply principles that protect participant welfare, fostering a research culture grounded in integrity and respect for human dignity.

elearn.title: INTERNATIONAL CYBER EDUCATION CENTRE
elearn.body1: The International Cyber Education Centre aims to provide healthcare professionals with the opportunity to improve educational degree through equal access to learning, regardless of location or time constraints.
elearn.body2: On December 9, 1996, by Order No. A/81 of the Rector of MNUMS, the Centre for Continuing Medical Education established the Doctoral and Master's Office, initiating graduate training domestically. Building on 28 years of experience in implementing graduate training, the International Cyber University of Medical Sciences (ICUMS) was established by Order No. B/35 of the Rector of MNUMS on September 7, 2015. When introducing the first Master of Science (online) Programme in Mongolia, the content, technology, and methodology were developed in collaboration with visiting professors Professor Young Moon Chae of Yonsei University in South Korea, an honorary professor at MNUMS, and Professor Meredith Praamsma of Johns Hopkins University, Maryland, USA. The programme was designed to align with international trends in higher education and was initially launched with two public health programmes. By 2016, it had expanded to six programmes.
elearn.list7: The Graduate School (GS) at MNUMS is responsible for graduate education, supported by 493 faculty members with doctoral degrees or higher. The International Cyber Education Centre (ICEC) ensures student-Centered, feedback-driven education. To elevate the master of science (online) programme to an international level, all course materials have been made available in both English and Mongolian to accommodate international students. The programme is delivered through the Moodle 3.11 platform, and continues to attract students from both abroad and all 21 provinces of Mongolia.
elearn.list8: Master of science (online) programmes are fully delivered through the Moodle 3.11 platform, in accordance with the rules and regulations governing graduate courses at MNUMS. The programmes are continuously updated to reflect current trends in higher education, as well as feedback and needs from stakeholders.

activity1.name: Orientation Training 1

activity4.timing: After passing the comprehensive exam and after the supervisors have been assigned.

activity5.name: Progress Discussions
activity5.purpose: Ensure proper project writing, prepare for defense, and monitor if faculties are effectively working with students.
activity5.timing: After completing the project writing and before the preliminary defense.

activity6.name: Journal Club
activity6.purpose: Enhance progress, choose appropriate topics, methods, and models, and improve presentation skills.
activity6.timing: Throughout the entire period leading up to the official defense.

elearn.text10: TRAINING SESSIONS AND MEETINGS FOR STUDENTS IN 2024
elearn.text11: When selecting the topic for their research project, Master's students are encouraged to base it on their personal experience as a professional, research interests, and needs for their growth. For example, they can develop a hypothesis and investigate issues or problems relevant to the area where they live or any specific topic of interest to them. These ensure the academic freedom over their research interests.
elearn.text12: After the annual admissions process, new students participate in an orientation training that combines in-person and online sessions. This training provides information on the course structure, usage of the learning system, project writing, and the defense process. For the Spring 2024 admissions, an additional session on research methodology was included in the orientation training.
elearn.text13: Courses at ICEC employ variety of digital learning and teaching methods as appropriate to engage students to the fullest extent.

masterNew.heading: MASTER
masterNew.title: ON-CAMPUS MASTER'S PROGRAMMES
masterNew.mainText: The Graduate school of MNUMS offers 11 programmes approved by the order of the Minister of Education and Science of Mongolia No.A/146 "Approval of name and indexes of programmes".
masterNew.mainText2: On-Campus master’s programme - The programme spans for 2 years (each academic year consists of 2 semesters).
masterNew.group51: A module for the Thesis Writing

list.item1: The academic year of master and doctoral studies consists of 2 semesters, spanning 16 weeks.
list.item2: Upon the request of the student who did not graduate on time due to the pre-approved reasons, the student can be considered for a one-year extension of graduation (one time) by the professional department, with the decision of the Division of graduate education policy and management.
list.item3: If students are transferred from other accredited programmes, it is possible to transfer the previously earned credits.
list.item4: The research topic and thesis must not be duplicated with other Master's or Doctoral thesis.
list.item5: During the study period, the students must achieve a score of 70% or higher to be considered as complete.
list.item6: The research topic, methods, a supervisor and an advisor, should be approved by the professional department.
list.item7: On-Campus master's students should be assigned with a supervisor and an advisor by the professional department.
list.item8: Ethics approval for the research must be obtained by submitting a request to the Research Ethics and Control Committee of MNUMS.
list.item9: Progressive review of research proposal: Organize discussion with professional experts to review the research proposal.
list.item10: Publish 2 research papers: Students must publish at least 2 research papers at the official Journal of MNUMS Health Sciences or other journals.
list.item11: Participate in a scientific conference (2 times): Students will present the research findings at academic conferences.
list.item12: Thesis will be written based on the research findings.
list.item13: Preliminary Defense: Organize a preliminary oral defense with at least 7 professional experts to review the research results.
list.item14: Final Defense: The thesis will be presented at the final oral defense consisting of the 11 members of the academic council approved by the Rector of MNUMS. Over 75% of the council members should approve to pass the final defense, according to the blind vote.

subjectList.subtitle: ONLINE MASTER'S PROGRAMMES
subjectList.item1: The International Cyber Education Centre of the Graduate School is the founder in Mongolia to initiate an online master's degree programme in the medical field. Our programme's implementation was an initiative that developed the first online master's programme in the medical field in Asia. Online master's programme provide a valuable opportunity for healthcare professionals living and working in Mongolian rural areas who can’t achieve on-campus master programme and over workloads to improve their educational degree and careers through flexible, accessible learning options.
subjectList.item2: The Graduate school of MNUMS offers the 6 online master’s programmes in accordance with the order No. A/147 of the Minister of Education and Science of Mongolia.
subjectList.item3: Online master’s programme - The programme spans for 2 years (each academic year consists of 2 semesters).
subjectList.item4: For more information about the Online Master's programme, please visit icec@mnum.edu.mn

doctorNew.title: DOCTORAL PROGRAMME
doctorNew.mainText: The Mongolian National University of Medical Sciences (MNUMS) offers 11 doctoral programmes, which consist of basic professional, professional, and research courses.
doctorNew.mainText2: The doctoral programme spans 3 years, with a total of 60 credit hours under the curriculum.

list.item12: 12 credit hours, including 6 credit hours on research methodology, ethics, and intellectual property

doctorNew.mainText3: In the doctoral programme, research topics and methods are reviewed and approved by the Professional Department of School and Academic Council. The ethical approval for research, which must prove that the ethical standards have been met in the process of conducting the research, will be reviewed by the Research Ethics and Control Committee of MNUMS.
doctorNew.mainText4: Upon the completion of the research, doctoral candidates are required to publish two papers in peer-reviewed journals indexed in Web of Science and Scopus as the  first author or corresponding author. The thesis will undergo a peer-review process, with the evaluation conducted by an expert approved by the Rector of MNUMS, and will proceed to both preliminary and final defenses.

scholarshipNew.title: Post-Doctoral Scholarship of MNUMS
scholarshipNew.description: The Post-Doctoral Scholarship of MNUMS supports researchers in leadership roles and the implementation of academic projects aligned with the MNUMS Academic Council. Ph.D. researchers selected for this scholarship will receive funding for 1-2 years, with the possibility of a one-year extension.

research.header: Key Features of the Post-Doctoral Scholarship:
research.framework1: Research Duration & Funding: Research funding is available for up to 1 year, with a possible extension of one additional year.
research.framework2: Collaboration: The scholarship supports both domestic and international research collaborations, including partnerships with universities and research organizations.
research.framework3: Research Location: The project must be conducted at MNUMS, utilizing its departments, laboratories, or hospitals.
research.framework4: Contract: A tripartite agreement between the researcher, their supervisor, and MNUMS will be signed, with progress reports submitted to the Graduate Education Policy and Management every six months.
research.framework5: Publication Requirement: Researchers must publish at least one article as the first author in a recognized journal (Web of Science, Scopus, or PubMed indexed).
research.framework6: Supervision: The researcher will supervise Master's and Doctoral students in their research and training.
research.framework7: Salary: The researcher will be compensated based on their academic rank.
research.framework8: Contract Violations: Any breach of the research contract will result in the reimbursement of all scholarship funds.

details.guideline_1: Applicants must be full-time faculty members at MNUMS with an academic rank of Associate Professor or higher.
details.guideline_2: Applicants must have prior experience leading research projects funded by MNUMS or other research organizations.

requirements.requirement_2: Ph.D. Certificate (must be awarded within the last 5 years)
requirements.requirement_3: Letter of Support from the Academic Council of MNUMS, confirming the research project's approval.
requirements.requirement_4: Research Proposal (outlining the project topic, objectives, and supporting documentation).

stipend_details.header: Ministry of Education’s Post-Doctoral Research Scholarship
stipend_details.description: The Post-Doctoral Research Scholarship, awarded by the Ministry of Education and Science, is designed to support Ph.D. graduates conducting research and innovation in science and technology. This scholarship aims to foster the next generation of scientists and researchers who are engaged in groundbreaking projects.
stipend_details.additional_info: Successful applicants will be awarded the Innovation Scholarship in the name of the Minister of Science and Technology.
stipend_details.application_procedure: The Ministry of Education, Culture, and Science oversees the management of the scholarship, and applicants must follow the procedures outlined in Minister's Order No. 222 (2011).
stipend_details.more_info1: For further details, visit the Ministry’s website:

scholarshipStudent.title: MNUMS Rector’s Research Scholarship
scholarshipStudent.description: The MNUMS Rector’s Research Scholarship provides funding for Master's and Doctoral students conducting research at MNUMS’s institutes, laboratories, and hospitals. Master's students can receive up to 3 million MNT, while Doctoral students can receive up to 5 million MNT. This funding is intended for research expenses such as purchasing reagents, equipment, software, and publishing costs. Up to 50 scholarships will be awarded to eligible students.

requirement.framework1: Applicants must be citizens of Mongolia.
requirement.framework2: Applicants must be enrolled in a Master's or Doctoral programme at MNUMS, with a minimum GPA of 3.2.
requirement.framework3: The research topic and methodology should be well-defined and discussed.
requirement.framework4: The applicant must not have transferred between departments, schools, or programmes.
requirement.framework5: Research must be conducted within a research institute, laboratory, or hospital at MNUMS.
requirement.framework6: The department or supervising professor must support the scholarship application.

requirement1.headerBody: The student applying for the scholarship must submit the following documents to the Division of Graduate Education Policy and Management.
requirement1.document4: Letter of Recommendation from the Supervisor
requirement1.document5: Research Proposal (1,000 words, including the research introduction, innovative aspects, importance, and progress)
requirement1.document6: Letter of Support from the Department and Professors
requirement1.document7: Approval Letter from the Director of the Institute, Laboratory Manager, or Hospital Chief at MNUMS
requirement1.document8: If applicable, a letter confirming the acceptance of an article for publication in a peer-reviewed journal (indexed in Web of Science, Scopus, or PubMed)

scholarshipTeacher.title: Research Grants of Science and Technology Support Fund for faculties, staffs, and researchers of MNUMS
scholarshipTeacher.description: The research projects are granted to the faculties, staffs and researchers of the current university, according to the order No. A/30 dated February 14, 2024 of the Rector of MNUMS.
scholarshipTeacher.text2: Within the framework of MNUMS's Development Policy-2030, research and development priorities, the university will bring the results of its research and development work to the level of the world’s best schools, and will cooperate with domestic and foreign academic institutions, expansion, establishment, and development of international-level research institutes and laboratories, and aims to increase the number of works published in international journals based on the results of research and development and to support the development of a research university through the creation and localization of new technologies, patents, and innovative products.

requirement.psl1: The project must be in accordance with the development policy of MNUMS and the priorities of academic work.
requirement.psl2: The research project should leverage the resources of MNUMS, including its research laboratories, centers, and institutes. The project should be aimed at strengthening clinical and basic research, foster innovation, training opportunities, and building external collaborations. Joint research projects are particularly advantageous.
requirement.psl3: The project leader should be a full-time teacher and researcher of MNUMS and have published works in international journals registered on the SCOPUS platform in the last 5 years, registered and protected intellectual property.
requirement.psl4: The project implementation team should include the participation of academicians of MNUMS, representatives of universities, and research institutes with foreign cooperation.
requirement.psl5: Model and statistical processing should be planned in accordance with the rules of research ethics and scientific methods.
requirement.psl6: Project budget and financial calculations should be realistic.
requirement.psl7: Completing market research and calculations needed for the economic circulation of new technologies, patents, and innovative works created because of the project is a must.
requirement.psl8: The project requires publishing an article in an international journal indexed in the SCOPUS platform under the name of MNUMS (responsible or 1st author).
requirement.psl9: The research project must be discussed and supported at the meeting of the Academic Council of constituent Schools.

admissionRequirements: Master's and Doctoral program applicants must meet the admission exam requirements developed by the Graduate School for the specific field of study.
Master's program applicants must have a bachelor's degree or equivalent, with a GPA of at least 2.5 (75%).
Doctoral program applicants must have a Master of Science degree or equivalent, with a GPA of at least 3.0 (80%).

If an applicant wishes to enroll in a Master's or Doctoral program in a different field than previously studied, they must complete prerequisite courses to ensure curriculum continuity. These courses are not counted toward the program's core credits.

Foreign students choosing a Mongolian-language program must meet Mongolian language proficiency requirements. Students under intergovernmental agreements must comply with the conditions specified in the agreement.

Foreign students choosing an English-language program must meet the admission exam requirements jointly developed by the Foreign Language Center and Graduate School. Additional requirements may be imposed based on the specific program content and characteristics, upon recommendation from the constituent school, professional department, or unit.

postgraduateCenterPage.admissionIntro: MNUMS Postgraduate Training Center accepts admissions in the following main fields to continuously improve the knowledge and skills of physicians and medical professionals.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType1Text: The postgraduate basic specialization residency training is designed for graduates with medical education and prepares trainees in specific specialization fields. Admission registration and related information are announced according to the designated program schedule.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType2Title: 2. Sub-specialization and Professional Development Training (Physicians and other medical professionals)
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType2Text: This level of training encompasses professionals from the following fields:
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType2ScopeText: Medical Doctors, Dental Doctors, Traditional Medicine Doctors, Pharmacists, Biomedical specialists, Public Health researchers.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType2TrainingTypeText: Sub-specialization and professional development training courses.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType3Title: 3. Sub-specialization and Professional Development Training (Nursing and other medical professionals)
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType3Text: The training package for nursing and mid-level professionals includes the following specializations:
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType3ScopeText: Nurses, Midwives, Pharmacist assistants, Lab technicians, Imaging technicians, Paramedics and related professionals.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionType3TrainingTypeText: Nursing sub-specialization and professional development training courses.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionScheduleText: Admissions are organized in spring and fall semesters annually. Spring semester registration is held in the 1st-3rd weeks of February, with training starting from March 1st.
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionRegText: Applicants register through the online system (https://admission.mnums.edu.mn).
postgraduateCenterPage.admissionBaseText: Training is conducted at MNUMS-affiliated and 74 base hospitals.
postgraduateCenterPage.graduationIntro: MNUMS Postgraduate Training Center organizes the issuance of professional certificates and diplomas to trainees who have successfully completed basic and sub-specialization training programs.
postgraduateCenterPage.graduationType1Text: Resident physicians enrolled in basic specialization training participate in the following graduation activities according to the training program:

graduationType1Steps.text: Report defense: Trainees conduct report defense according to the established schedule and have their training progress evaluated.

Grade advancement exam: Every 2 semesters, trainees take grade advancement exams according to the Postgraduate Activities Regulation. Those who fail may take academic leave and continue in the next academic year.

Research work: Resident physicians must present at MNUMS or international scientific conferences, discuss clinical cases, or publish articles in MNUMS or international scientific journals during their training.

Graduation exam: Trainees who have completed the curriculum and met all requirements are authorized to take the graduation exam by the MNUMS Rector's order. The exam includes theoretical and clinical skills components. A score of 80 or above is considered passing.
Graduation documents: Trainees who successfully pass the report defense and graduation exam receive a basic specialization diploma.

postgraduateCenterPage.graduationType2Text: Medical Doctors, Dental Doctors, Traditional Medicine Doctors, Pharmacists, Biomedical specialists, and Public Health researchers' graduation information is covered in this section.

postgraduateCenterPage.subspecNote: MNUMS Sub-specialization training fields per Ministry of Health Order A/445 dated 2021.07.07:
postgraduateCenterPage.hospitalsIntro: Postgraduate training activities are organized not only at MNUMS-affiliated hospitals but also at contracted state and private hospitals. The training fields and number of trainees at each contracted hospital are regulated according to agreements between MNUMS and the contracted hospitals, depending on the resources and capacity of each hospital. The MNUMS Vice-Rector for Hospital Affairs signs cooperation agreements with contracted hospitals annually.
